caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: ACEC/BST evidence spans engineering and design services, member firms and a small BST subsample rather than NAICS 541330 LMM employers.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions measure replaceable task share, not adoption, pricing or terminal demand.
inputs.pi_dist: No source found quantifying 541330 revenue by public cost-based, fixed-fee or private contract type.
inputs.pi_dist: FAR/FHWA mechanisms apply to relevant government-assisted work, not every engineering engagement.
inputs.pi_moat: ACEC describes differentiation and integrated data/workflows but does not measure NAICS 541330 retained savings, prices or switching rates.

inputs.pi_moat: The estimate excludes licensure, human review, responsible charge, backlog and terminal-demand survival mechanisms; competitive bids and broadly available AI tools may reduce retention faster than the range implies.
inputs.t50_years: The source reports expectations and strategy, not a longitudinal NAICS-541330 effective-adoption series.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: This is AI-strategy prevalence, used as a conservative proxy for current effective adoption; it is not direct task-level adoption.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: ACEC member-firm survey is broader than NAICS 541330 and may not represent LMM firms.
inputs.historical_analogs: No quantified historical analog was selected; generative AI may diffuse faster than prior software waves.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No directly observed 60-plus percentage was available in the accessible source.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: AEC scope includes architecture, construction and environmental consulting beyond 541330.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: This is a qualitative documentation judgment, not a measured shortage rate.
inputs.active_consolidators: Observed count is a short 2025 window and ACEC-member subset, not an all-market annual consolidator census.
inputs.buy_mult: The selected range is third-party guidance aggregating A/E and its underlying transaction data are not public.
inputs.buy_mult: Reported small-firm earnings may be SDE or pre-bonus profit; use market-rate owner compensation before applying EBITDA multiples.
inputs.exit_mult: No transaction-level LMM 541330 exit-comparable set was available.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit multiple requires a larger, transferable platform and should not be assumed solely from sector deal volume.
inputs.pricing_structure: No quantified NAICS-541330 contract-type split was found.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $260,281 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541330); class <5: 27,901 firms, avg revenue/firm $412,809 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $64,605 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 6,267 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,523,903 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $238,491 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 4,702 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,089,183 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $483,457 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 4,840 firms, avg revenue/firm $9,897,160 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,548,906 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 1,297 firms, avg revenue/firm $42,835,429 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,703,745 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 685 firms, avg revenue/firm $252,830,867 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $39,568,031 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 6,137

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Role-level estimate anchored to ACEC's reported use cases and expected task automation; technical deployment remains uneven and human review/field work are excluded.

l.detail: BLS Industry Productivity (IPS) direct: labor compensation (L02) $172,759.1M / sectoral output (T30) $367,702.1M, year 2024

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: All deterministic mechanics, exact-byte digests, identities, route, dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering, and arithmetic pass. Reopened sources support the useful direction of AI task compression, partial savings pass-through, limited workflow/data capture, adoption timing, active A/E acquisition, valuation ranges, and durable accountable demand. The record separates licensure and responsible review as terminal-demand mechanisms from pi_moat, so no score-changing terminal/capture double count remains. Publication nevertheless requires the authored caveats because several score inputs rely on disclosed broader-AEC proxies, unquantified contract mix and capture, an unobserved owner-age/shortage estimate, and adviser or paywalled valuation evidence.

sources.title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in the Engineering Industry
sources.what_it_supports: ACEC's 2025 member/industry evidence on AI strategy, use cases, expected task automation, staffing expectations, uneven deployment and 3–5 year transformation expectations; it also describes firm-specific data, AI/business-system integration and client differentiation through faster or higher-value delivery.

sources.title: FAR 31.105 Construction and architect-engineer contracts
sources.what_it_supports: Federal A/E cost principles, reimbursable-cost and indirect-rate negotiation mechanisms relevant to savings pass-through on applicable contracts.

sources.title: FHWA Consultant Services - Design
sources.what_it_supports: FHWA's current reference to the AASHTO Uniform Audit & Accounting Guide and training for A/E consultant indirect-cost-rate development and oversight.

sources.title: Headline: Big Buyers, Sellers Reshape Industry M&A
sources.what_it_supports: 2025 ACEC/Morrissey Goodale reporting on deal pace, repeat acquirers, PE participation and A/E specialty demand.

sources.title: Zweig Group releases 2026 Principals, Partners + Owners Report of AEC Firms
sources.what_it_supports: Existence and scope of a current AEC principal/ownership-transition benchmark, and reported ownership transition context; it does not state a 60-plus share.

sources.title: Architecture and Engineering Firm Valuation: What's Your A&E Firm Worth in 2026?
sources.what_it_supports: Directional LMM A/E normalized-EBITDA multiple ranges, size tiers, principal-dependence diligence and normalization cautions; third-party buy-side guide, not transaction-level primary data.

sources.title: 2025 Valuation Report of AEC Firms
sources.what_it_supports: Current AEC valuation survey scope, period and methodology; detailed multiples are paywalled and not directly used as numeric observations.

title: Engineering Services
